title: penstemon sepalulus
intro: Penstemon sepalulus, also known as Mexican penstemon, is a species of flowering plant in the plantain family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is said to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and soothing digestive problems. The herbal preparations of Penstemon sepalulus include making a tea by steeping the leaves in hot water, which can be used to treat various ailments, or making a tincture by infusing the plant in a solvent to create a concentrated extract.
